front: Photograph: depicts Florence Owens Thompson gazing into the distance with two children burying their faces against her back, taken at a pea-pickers camp in Nipomo, California
Photograph: later retouched to remove the subject's thumb from the lower-right corner
Photographer: the subject of her most famous photograph, Florence Owens Thompson, was Cherokee
Photograph: early reproductions accompanied by a Paul Taylor caption that incorrectly stated the subject had sold a tent to get food
Photograph: included in Edward Steichen's Family of Man exhibition at MoMA
Photograph: official title refers to 'Human Erosion in California'
Photograph: shows a man in a beat-up hat with a cup, clasping his hands and facing away from a breadline at a San Francisco soup kitchen run by the 'White Angel'
Photographer: photographed Japanese American children saying the Pledge of Allegiance before being interned, documenting Manzanar for the War Relocation Authority
Photographer: over 800 of her photographs of a Japanese American internment camp were impounded by the WRA
Photographer: photographed a flagpole rising between rows of identical barracks at Manzanar
Photographer: unlike Ansel Adams, was able to document both early and late stages of internment at Manzanar
Photographer: photographed a man hoisting his grandson on his back and an American flag in front of barracks for the War Relocation Authority
Photographer: took photos of Mount Williamson overlooking Manzanar in 1942
Photograph: collaborative photo-essay with economist Paul Schuster Taylor documenting Dust Bowl displacement, featuring photographs of empty roads
Photograph: collaboration with Pirkle Jones documenting the flooding of Monticello, California under Lake Berryessa; published as an issue of Aperture magazine
Photographer: worked for the Farm Security Administration photographing the Great Depression; best known for a photograph of Florence Owens Thompson
Photographer: April 2020 MoMA online exhibition titled 'Words and Pictures' paired her photographs with poetry
Photographer: took pictures of the hands and feet of Indonesian dancers while accompanying Paul Taylor on a trip to Asia
Photographer: a documentary quotes her desire to go down to the street and 'grab a hunk of lightning'
Photographer: used connections at Life magazine to help fund Edward Steichen's Family of Man exhibit
Photographer: late-career series captured the work of Public Defenders in California
Photographer: her photograph of young men on a porch was transformed into a propaganda poster reading 'This is your America... Keep it free!'
Photographer: showed three men from Vermont leaning on a car next to a tent in a work captioned 'Little Money'
Photographer: photographed a 'Resettled farm child from Taos, New Mexico' resting her head in her hands
Photographer: photographed the 1934 West Coast Waterfront Strike, including a woman holding a newspaper reading 'Cleveland, are you there?'
Photographer: took Waiting for Relief Checks in Calipatria, California during the Great Depression
